title: "setDaemon", "setPriority" and "getThreadGroup" should not be invoked on virtual threads
description: Invoking "setDaemon", "setPriority" or "getThreadGroup" on virtual threads is not supported and will throw an UnsupportedOperationException. This rule detects such invocations to prevent runtime errors.
recommendation: Do not call setDaemon, setPriority, or getThreadGroup on virtual threads. Use supported thread operations only.
impact: Calling these methods on virtual threads will result in exceptions, potentially causing application failures.

noncompliant:
Thread vThread = Thread.ofVirtual().start(() -> {});
vThread.setDaemon(true); // Noncompliant
vThread.setPriority(5); // Noncompliant
vThread.getThreadGroup(); // Noncompliant
compliant:
Thread vThread = Thread.ofVirtual().start(() -> {});
// Do not call setDaemon, setPriority, or getThreadGroup on vThread.

checks.message: Do not call setDaemon, setPriority, or getThreadGroup on virtual threads. Use supported thread operations only.
